CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Julie L. Fieber, of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer, APC, Tel. 415.397.2800, for defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS PAUL PISCOPO, AND MG-101 CAL, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is granted. Absent a viable defense to enforcement, the Court must grant a motion to compel arbitration pursuant to CCP Sec. 1281.2 if it determines there is an agreement to arbitrate that has not been waived or revoked. [CCP Sec. 1281.2(a),(b); Cinel v. Barna (2012) 206 CA4th 1383, 1389, 142 CR3d 329, 334.] Here, Plaintiff signed a valid arbitration agreement. The court finds Plaintiff had the opportunity to review the agreement and a translator was available to her. [See Decl. of Adriana Morales.] Under CCP Sec. 1281.2, a court may deny a motion to compel arbitration when a party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action with a third party that arises out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and presents a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. Henry v. Alcove Inv., Inc., (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 94. Here, the allegations against all Defendants stem from the same series of events and all issues may be heard in arbitration. Plaintiff has not established that Defendants have waived their right to compel arbitration. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
